tasks.description: Create Worker project scaffold with wrangler.toml and D1 binding
steps: worker-bookmarks/ directory exists with src/index.js and wrangler.toml
wrangler.toml has d1_databases binding named DB
wrangler.toml has worker name 'bookmarks'
wrangler dev starts without errors

tasks.description: Create D1 schema (bookmarks, tags, bookmark_tags tables) and seed 14 approved tags
steps: bookmarks table exists with columns: id, url, title, note, is_read, created_at, updated_at
tags table exists with columns: id, name, is_approved
bookmark_tags junction table exists with foreign keys
14 seed tags inserted with is_approved = 1
url has UNIQUE constraint on bookmarks table

tasks.description: CORS handling and API key auth middleware
steps: OPTIONS requests return correct CORS headers for https://jeffrey-lean.com and localhost
Write endpoints (POST, PATCH, DELETE) return 401 without valid Authorization header
Write endpoints succeed with valid Bearer token
GET endpoints work without auth

tasks.description: POST /bookmarks — create bookmark with auto title fetch
steps: POST with valid URL creates a bookmark in D1 and returns id, url, title, created_at
Title is auto-fetched from the URL's <title> tag
If title fetch fails, bookmark is still saved with URL as fallback title
Duplicate URL returns 409 error
Invalid URL returns 422 error

tasks.description: GET /bookmarks — list with filtering, search, and pagination
steps: Returns paginated bookmark list with tags array joined from bookmark_tags
?tag=rust filters to bookmarks with that tag
?search=term searches across title and note fields
?untagged=true returns only bookmarks with no tags
?unread=true returns only bookmarks where is_read = 0
?limit=N&offset=M paginates correctly
Response includes total count

tasks.description: PATCH /bookmarks/:id — update bookmark fields and tags
steps: Updates note field when provided
Updates is_read field when provided
Replaces bookmark's tags when tags array provided
Tags not in approved list are created with is_approved = 0
Returns 404 for non-existent bookmark id
Requires auth

tasks.description: DELETE /bookmarks/:id — delete a bookmark
steps: Deletes bookmark and its bookmark_tags entries
Returns 404 for non-existent id
Requires auth

tasks.description: Tag endpoints — GET /tags, POST /tags, POST /tags/approve
steps: GET /tags returns { approved: [...], pending: [...] } grouped by is_approved
POST /tags creates a new tag with is_approved = 1 (requires auth)
POST /tags/approve sets is_approved = 1 for a pending tag (requires auth)
Duplicate tag name returns appropriate error

tasks.description: POST /bookmarks/batch — bulk update for CLI import
steps: Accepts { updates: [{ id, tags, note, is_read }] } payload
Updates each bookmark's tags, note, is_read as provided
New tags created as is_approved = 0 (pending)
Returns { updated: N } with count of successfully updated bookmarks
Requires auth

tasks.description: Create Hugo content file and layout template shell
steps: content/bookmarks/_index.md exists with title 'Bookmarks' and layout 'bookmarks'
layouts/bookmarks/list.html exists and inherits from baseof.html via {{ define "main" }}
Page renders at /bookmarks/ with hugo server

tasks.description: JS: fetch and render bookmark list from Worker API
steps: Page fetches bookmarks from site.Params.bookmarkApiUrl on load
Each bookmark displays: title (linked to URL), tags as chips, note (if exists), date saved
Bookmarks sorted newest first
Read/unread status is NOT rendered on the page
Loading state shown while fetching

tasks.description: JS: tag filter chips fetched from /tags endpoint
steps: Approved tags rendered as clickable chips at top of page
Clicking a tag filters the bookmark list to only that tag
Active tag visually highlighted
Clicking active tag clears the filter

tasks.description: JS: free-text search across titles and notes
steps: Search input field present on page
Typing triggers filtered fetch via ?search= query param
Search works in combination with tag filter

tasks.description: CSS: tag chips, search bar, bookmark list — match hugo-blog-awesome theme
steps: Tag chips styled consistently with theme's dark/light mode
Search bar matches theme input styling
Bookmark list items use existing .post-item patterns where applicable
Page looks correct in both dark and light mode

tasks.description: Rust project scaffold with clap, reqwest, serde dependencies
steps: bookmark-cli/ directory with Cargo.toml
Dependencies: clap (derive), reqwest (json, blocking or tokio), serde (derive), serde_json, toml
cargo build succeeds

tasks.description: Config file loading from ~/.config/bookmark/config.toml
steps: Reads api_url and api_key from ~/.config/bookmark/config.toml
Prints helpful error if config file missing
Prints helpful error if api_url or api_key missing

tasks.description: bookmark list subcommand with --untagged, --unread, --tag filters
steps: bookmark list prints bookmarks in readable table/list format
bookmark list --untagged shows only untagged
bookmark list --unread shows only unread
bookmark list --tag rust filters by tag
Displays id, title, tags, date for each bookmark

tasks.description: bookmark add <url> subcommand
steps: bookmark add https://example.com posts URL to API
Prints created bookmark (id, title) on success
Prints error on duplicate or invalid URL

tasks.description: bookmark update <id> subcommand with --tags, --note, --read flags
steps: bookmark update 1 --tags 'rust,networking' sends PATCH with tags
bookmark update 1 --note 'great article' sends PATCH with note
bookmark update 1 --read sends PATCH with is_read=true
Flags can be combined in single command
Prints updated bookmark on success

tasks.description: bookmark tags subcommand — list, approve, add
steps: bookmark tags lists approved and pending tags separately
bookmark tags approve <tag> calls POST /tags/approve
bookmark tags add <tag> calls POST /tags
Prints confirmation on success

tasks.description: bookmark export --untagged --json for agent consumption
steps: Outputs JSON array of {id, url, title, created_at} to stdout
--untagged flag filters to untagged bookmarks only
Output is valid JSON parseable by external tools
Can be piped to file: bookmark export --untagged --json > batch.json

tasks.description: bookmark import <file.json> for batch updates from agent
steps: Reads JSON array of {id, tags, note} from file
Sends POST /bookmarks/batch with updates
Prints count of updated bookmarks on success
Prints error if file is invalid JSON

tasks.description: Add Bookmarks menu entry and bookmarkApiUrl param to config.toml
steps: menu.main has Bookmarks entry with url '/bookmarks/' and weight 25
params.bookmarkApiUrl set to https://bookmarks.leanwf1117.workers.dev
Bookmarks appears in site navigation between Posts and About

tasks.description: Deploy Worker to Cloudflare and verify end-to-end
steps: Worker deployed and accessible at https://bookmarks.leanwf1117.workers.dev
D1 database created and schema applied
API key set via wrangler secret put
Hugo site builds with bookmarks page
Bookmarks page loads and fetches from live Worker API

tasks.description: Write HTTP Shortcuts app setup guide for Android bookmark capture
steps: Document lists: install HTTP Shortcuts, create shortcut with POST method
Includes Worker URL, Authorization header, and JSON body format
Mentions enabling 'Show in Share Sheet'
Tested: sharing a URL from Android browser triggers the shortcut and saves to D1

patterns: Existing view counter Worker uses KV, simple routing, CORS allow-list pattern in worker/src/index.js
Hugo layouts use {{ define "main" }} inheriting from theme baseof.html
View counter JS: inline <script> at page bottom, Worker URL from site.Params, fetch + DOM update
Theme: hugo-blog-awesome with dark mode, CSS classes: .post-item, .post-item-title, .post-item-meta
keyFiles: worker/src/index.js — existing Worker pattern to reference
layouts/_default/list.html — existing list page with view counter JS
layouts/partials/postCard.html — post card component to mirror for bookmarks
config.toml — site config, menu entries, params
nonGoals: No admin web UI — curation via CLI + agent only
No SSR/SEO optimization — client-side rendered page
CLI does not embed any LLM — agent orchestration is external
